🎈   A Memory Book   🎈
Happy 70th Birthday,
Robin Slattery!
Natick's Director of Every Operation Everywhere
DJ Slatts — Live from Wherever She's Needed!

For decades, you have been the heart of every room you've walked into — as a friend, a neighbor, a mother figure, a colleague, and yes, a very enthusiastic DJ. Schools, rinks, fields, beaches, and living rooms — wherever you go, people feel it. This book is from all of us.

104
Messages of Love
70
Incredible Years
∞
Natick Forever

Inside This Book

🎈 Tributes & Quick Thoughts 🎈 A Poem for Robin 🎈 In the Spotlight 🎈 From the Kids 🎈 Parents & Families 🎈 Colleagues & Co-Workers 🎈 From Friends & Family

Can’t stop the feeling by Justin Timberlake will always remind me of you. I will always smile when I hear it🩷

Written with Love

A Poem for Robin

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈
✦ For Robin Slatts ✦
The first thought that comes to mind, When thinking of Robin Slatts, Is that she defines what kindness is, And there’s no disputing that. Her gentle way with all the kids, Her smile and sense of humor, Made all her days at Johnson school, The best for those who knew her. Age they say is just a number, But 70 is quite a milestone, The goal is to make it look fab like you do, Thank you for setting the beautiful tone! Robin you are a friend to cherish Thinking of others is what you do To be your friend is truly a gift And today we celebrate you! Love you GF 💕 Joyce
— Joyce MacGregor
Stories That Say It Best

In the Spotlight

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈
Featured Memory
Jordan Hoffman
One of my favorite memories of Mrs. Slattery—also known as DJ Slatts to many at Johnson—takes me back to a cold winter day when the snow began to fall. In the middle of the day, we decided to have a little fun by creating the Johnson School Snowflake Trivia game which we broadcasted over the loudspeaker like a radio show. Mrs. Slattery got on the loudspeaker and announced with full flair: "This is DJ Slatts coming to you live from Johnson Elementary School! Get ready for our snowflake trivia challenge—cool prizes are up for grabs! Teachers and Jaguars, grab your phones and dial 2200 with the correct answer. Remember, the 5th caller with the right answer wins—so get ready to ring in and claim your prize!" As the calls started pouring in, DJ Slatts was right there at the phone, spinning into action like a pro on the airwaves. “Hello, Johnson Elementary, this is DJ Slatts—are you the lucky caller?” she’d ask with perfect timing, each response delivered with that signature mix of flair and humor. And of course, when it wasn’t the 5th caller, she’d laugh and say, “Sorry, not this time—keep dialing, Jaguars!” We laughed endlessly, but what stayed with me most was the joy it brought to the whole school—one of those simple, spontaneous moments that made the day so joyful. This memory captures Mrs. Slattery perfectly—bringing warmth, laughter, and connection to everyone around her. Happy 70th birthday, Mrs. Slattery! Your generosity, care and love have made a difference in the lives of everyone around you.
College Essay
The Bianchi Family
Mrs. Slattery makes every day better! She is a positive light in every aspect of life….so much that college essays are written about her…find below an easy written by John At Johnson School, I have gained so many valuable experiences and encountered many inspirational teachers that it feels impossible to choose just one. I even wrote my college essay about how Johnson was life-changing because of its strong community, righteous values, and especially the Peacemakers program. At the center of it all is the heart of Johnson: Mrs. Slattery. Every morning and afternoon, you could find Mrs. Slattery at the doors, greeting students with a big smile or sending us off with encouragement. She somehow knew where every student needed to be at any moment, whether it was reminding someone about an afternoon playdate, managing traffic control on the blacktop, or going out of her way to give my younger sisters a sticker. She kept everything running so smoothly, yet she always made time for people. Mrs. Slattery embodies every value Johnson stands for. She is kind, hardworking, and deeply committed to building community. She worked closely with the teachers and principal and played a huge role in supporting the Peacemakers, helping create an environment that felt truly unique compared to the other elementary schools. Johnson wasn’t just a school; it was a family, and she helped make it that way.  She was the face of the Jaguar community, and helped the motto, “Once a Jaguar, always a Jaguar" be meaningful to me. Johnson built the foundation for my life, and Mrs. Slattery represents that foundation, one I will carry with me to college and beyond.
DJ Slats on the Blacktop
Laney George
How the children loved when DJ slats brought her rocking tunes to the Johnson blacktop. That was always a special day. Especially during Covid when everything was so scary. DJ slats in her wig spinning cotton-eyed Joe made everything seem more normal. Another fond memory is how Robin celebrated every occasion to the the max with balloons 🎈 and decorations! Every day was a special occasion at Johnson because of Robin. Lastly , and most importantly, was the kindness and care she gave to every student , staff member and parent. Robin made everyone feel special and welcome 🙏 what a gift she is to the whole Natick community ❤️ Happy Birthday Robin- you are so loved 🥰
From a Colleague
Ann McAvinn
Robin, you were truly the heart and soul of Johnson. You wore many hats—and wore them all with grace, humor, and heart. You greeted everyone with a smile that instantly made the school feel warm and welcoming. You were our “DJ Slatts,” always playing music—whether it was a holiday or just an ordinary day—and somehow turning the office into a place of joy, complete with spontaneous dance parties. You were also a counselor, comforting children who came in for a Band-Aid or worried about a forgotten lunch, and supporting staff as they navigated life’s challenges. You were our biggest cheerleader, celebrating the academic successes of students and even the accomplishments of staff members’ children. I’ll never forget you going out of your way to attend my son’s playoff game in another town—that’s just who you are. You were also an incredible organizer and connector. You were my agent- Sending me to different classes to do Irish dancing on St. Patricks Day, arranging for alumni to return and read to our students, and leading the way with graduation. You were the fixer, always helping me with my constant computer questions. And somehow, in the midst of it all, you were always ready with the best recommendations—flowers, restaurants, and everything in between. Because of you, Johnson wasn’t just a school—it was a community filled with warmth, care, and connection. It was as special as it was because of you. Happy Birthday, my friend. You have touched so many lives, and we are all better because of you.
Brown School
Chris Crosby
We were lucky to have Robin here at Brown last year! She brought so much cheer to the students and staff as DJ Slats, baking for staff, decorating for every holiday and I could just go on and on.... She was such a pleasure to have her here with us all!
The Birthday Surprise
Brenna Turner
My favorite memory was when Robin printed pictures of me and hung them all over the school for my 40th birthday. There were signs everywhere and of course balloons. She sure knows how to make everyone feel extra special!
The Director of Everything
Catherine Brunell
I love Mrs. Slattery as DJSlats but I love her even more as the Director of Every Operation Everywhere. She knew where the paper plates where - and probably had a rough estimate of how many there were in the basement PTO dungeon and maybe even by color. She knew who still had a lunch order to turn in and that your Cori was going to be invalid in 3 days. Robin also knew when you needed a hug and when you needed to hear a really funny story. I was recently organizing a group of high schoolers to come to the State House with us on a field trip and learned I needed a staff member. Without even thinking about it, I said, "Can I have Mrs. Slattery?" She is the backbone, the heart, the soul, the keeper of all memories and the unleasher of fun. And, she will make sure a bouncy house is delivered on time and that they leave the right length of cord to be plugged into exactly the outlet she has already determined would be best. God, I love her. She gets the kind of stuff done that makes your heart happy, your day easier and increases your hope that it is possible to get all of the little things done. Happy Birthday Robin!
Across the Years
Matt Brunell
I have so many warm memories of being buzzed into Johnson School—on my way to a teacher meeting, about to volunteer, or just dropping off a field trip permission slip—and there was Mrs. Slattery… always smiling, fully present, attentive to whatever you needed, even though you knew she was managing a thousand things in the main office. She carried herself with a certain grace in that nerve center of the school. And she took that ever-present grace and magnified it a thousandfold during the years of the Johnson closure. She held that school—and all of our hearts—together as we mourned, celebrated, and said goodbye. Here's to DJ Slats, who helped make the music of the school.
Once a Jaguar, Always a Jaguar

From the Kids

The kids whose mornings were made better, whose tears you dried, whose names you never forgot — and who are still out there carrying a little bit of Robin with them.

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈

Everytime Mrs. Slattery went on the PA announcements as DJ Slatts, I knew it was going to be a great day :)

Mrs. Slattery always made my day as DJ Slats and she always gave me the best hugs when I needed them.

🎂
One day I was at snack and it was Covid so we were all split up on dots outside. Everyone was super bored so Mrs.Slattery came out as DJ Slatz and started playing music. Everyone loved it and started dancing. Even though we had to zoom classes 10 minutes later everyone was in a better mood.
— Anonymous
⭐
Dear Mrs. Slattery, I hope you have the best birthday ever, you deserve it. You’ve always made Johnson the best place it could possibly be. You put a smile on anyone and everyone’s face. I’m glad that you’re doing that with Wilson now too. Best wishes and happy birthday. Sincerely, Mae Casey-Magleby
— Anonymous
🎈
DJ Slats would brighten my day by smiling at me when I walked into school. She was and still is SO funny.Once A Jaguar always a jaguar.Loved your jam tracks and how you set up dance party's on the blacktop and played songs from different years or cultures music or play song requests.Thank you for all you do/did to make kids smile! Happy birthday DJ slats -George Newman
— The Newmans

You’re “really really nice” (Hudson, 5th Grade now). We hope you have a great birthday!!

The best part of mornings at Johnson was being welcomed in by the ever-smiling Mrs. Slattery. Looking back as a high school senior, I still miss those hugs!

🌟
Dear Mrs. Slattery, Happy Birthday! Thank you for being with me all throughout elementary. One of my favorite parts of going to school was seeing you every morning. I will always remember you and I hope that you have a great birthday celebration.
— Doris and Max Yu
🥳
Happy birthday! You were a great member of the Johnson community. It was always so fun when you were DJ Slatts. And it was nice to still get to see you at Kennedy last year. I hope you have a great day!
— Owen Ahuja
💐
Happy birthday, DJ Slats! You will always be one of the liveliest teachers I have ever met. You always brought life to Johnson and made everyone excited for DJ Slats' beats! I hope your birthday is so much fun!
— Asha Sparkes (Johnson class of 2022)
🎊
In 4th grade, I was sick on the day the yearbook designs were due. I was so sad because my older sister had won the competition when she was in 4th grade and I thought I wasn't even going to be able to submit an entry. When I got better, I took my design to school and Mrs. Slattery greeted me with a big hug, took my design even though it was late and told me she was going to be sure it got into the contest. I didn't win, but I will always remember Mrs. Slattery for what she did that day.
— Ross Brunell
✨
When it was field day and she was the dj
— Logan Casali
🎶
Thank you for always being so kind and caring, you always made my day. And you were a great DJ
— Logan Casali
💛
Mrs.slattery made my day better by being kind.
— Will Casali
🌸
Dear Mrs. slattery, you are such a kind person. I wish you where at brown school. happy birthday!
— Will Casali
🎁
I always remember Ms. Slattery always being happy and ready to help lift people up. I also always remember her being very festive at holidays and always ready as DJ Slats to play some music and party.
— Ila Reed
🎵
Mrs. Slattery at the Johnson doors or playing music as DJ Slatts are some of my first memories of Johnson. I would be on the blacktop with Maggie and Vivi waiting for my brothers and she was always ready to greet us with a smile. It made me so excited for kindergarten.
— Molly Pollock
🤗
Mrs. Slattery was so welcoming all the many many times I was sent to the principal's office, and now she still comes to see me perform in shows around town!
— Will Pollock
The front steps of Johnson. Where every morning began and every kid was loved.
The Johnson Families

Parents & Families

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈
🎵
I’ll never forget the day I came to you for my first CORI check. I handed you my license and 3 year old Connor said very loudly “Mom is that a gun license?” (To this day I have no idea where he got that idea from!!). I was beyond mortified…but you gave this great laugh and never missed a beat… making me feel immediately at ease! You always greet every child by name and even know their siblings’ names! You made Johnson a warm welcoming place from the second we walked in the door. You were such an important part of what made Johnson so magical. We are forever grateful for the countless ways you supported our family and you helped feel part of the Johnson community starting on day 1! Love, Alicia, Mike, Addy, Connor and Katie Lynch
— The Lynch Family (Alicia, Mike, Addy, Connor and Katie)
🤗
Her dress up for spirit days and holidays. Shes very kind.
— Galicia family
🎂
Dear Mrs. Slattery, Thank you for you endless compassion, empathy, sympathy, kindness, and ability to put on a smile and help during difficult times. You managed to make every mom feel like their kids had another mother-figure available, which is priceless. You are the embodiment of love and generosity. Any person who knows you, is better for it. Thank you for the many times you offered your ears, your validation, and sound advice. The world is in your debt - and I know you're reading this right now shaking your head not agreeing, but I guarantee there are 10,000 more agreeing this is all true. Wishing you the very best of birthdays - you deserve everything your heart desires.
— Anonymous
⭐
Mrs. Slattery's dedication to the Johnson School Community was unmatched. The support she provided to staff, parents and students was extraordinary. She has so many wonderful traits, but what stands out above the rest is her sense of humor, her wonderful laugh and her pure love for the kids. It is a privilege to know her, and we feel so blessed that Will and Ben were able to experience her kindness and encouragement.
— The Wood Family
🎈
You always greet us with a smile! Johnson school days were the greatest and you always supported my family. You even supported my cheese business during Covid! ;) Always a pleasure to work with you! Happy Birthday! Love and hugs, Morgan Fulton (Fitzgerald Fam)
— ANONYMOUS
💕
Mrs. Slattery, Thank you for always showing up with a smile! Your presence has been felt in Natick for decades. We are so grateful to know you and still see you at the rink and around tonight. We wish you a beautiful birthday and year ahead!
— The Whalen Family
🎉
My favorite memory will always be Mrs Slattery greeting us with music and such amazing energy. She made me and my boys feel so loved. Finn was allowed to come explore Johnson in August so he could learn the school. Mrs Slattery, Buddy and Jordan Hoffman were the only ones in the building. They made us feel safe and we never left without a high five which soon turned into hugs. Mrs Slattery is such a bright light and we love her so much
— Shannon, Tim, Mack and Finn
🌟
Dear Mrs. Slattery, Thank you. Thank you for all the love you give my family. Thank you for helping me make it through kindergarten and for always giving me the time to run something by you. I don’t even know that you are aware of all your special qualities that make you one of the most amazing women I know because it’s just you being you. Thank you for teaching me how to use a Mac book and giving me all those lessons. Thank you for always reminding me that I am a good mom and advocate when I needed it most. I love you so much
— Shannon, Tim, Mack and Finn
🥳
We love you DJ Slatts! Thanks for all you did for the Jaguars. You are a Johnson Icon. We hope you have a very Happy Birthday. Sending big birthday hugs! XO The Brockmans. Ali, Scott, Emme and Bess
— The Brockmans Ali, Scott, Emme & Bess
💐
We have so many memories of Mrs. Slattery during her years at Johnson. All three boys said their favorite memories were Mrs. Slattery playing awesome music, all of the cool wiggling heads on her desk and how she was always so nice to everyone. For me, Mrs. Slattery was a partner in crime during my years on PTO - always knowing who to call, the forms to send in and how to get things done. Thank you for everything you've done for our family. You are a change maker in so many people's lives!
— The Grant family
🎊
Mrs. Slattery, we hope you have a very wonderful birthday! We have so many great memories of you, it’s hard to think of just one instance, but the way your smile always felt like a hug is a feeling we will always have. We hope to see you around soon!
— The Hoeys
✨
Happy birthday, Robin! I hope you are surrounded by the people you love and lots of balloons on your special day. I found it difficult to pick out one memory to share with you here because you are such an integral part of all my memories of Johnson. So instead, I'll say thank you. Thank you for being welcoming and enthusiastic and caring and still managing to stay on top of every detail. Thank you for answering about a million of my PTO-related questions. Thank you for being the life of the party as DJ Slatts--the kids loved it, and I loved walking up to drop-off in the morning and hearing your tunes blaring. Best way to start the day! Thank you for caring so much about the students -- and the entire Johnson community. You cheered us on, kept us on track, made us laugh, brought balloons to every celebration, and made every person you talked to feel like family. What a gift! Thank you. Eileen Ahuja
— The Ahuja Family
🎶
Dear Robin, How lucky are the Nevilles, who got to be welcomed by you at Johnson Elementary for 9 years straight! Your joy and love for Johnson and all the kids was clearly seen and felt by all of us. Michael & Andrew will forever remember your smiling face and interactions in the main office and around the school! I know I am grateful for all the help you gave me when I was a parent volunteering with PTO , doing the Johnson Auction and the Holiday Bazaar, to name a few of the events. How many cakes for the cake walks did we bake? LOTS OF THEM! DJ Slatts always provided the tunes. Extra bonus: Working at Wilson with you!! Extra lucky! Thank you for all the love and enthusiasm you bring every single day! We love and appreciate you! Love, Kate Neville
— The Neville Family
💛
Robin, You always had a warm welcome for every student & parent, you try to find the good in everyone. You were always loved and apprciated by the MacBlane family. I love that I get to continue to work with you at the High School games. We would like to wish you a special Birhtday🎂 ❤️ 🎈🎈We hope you feel special today and always because you are!!!! XO
— The MacBlane family
🌸
Dear Mrs. Slattery, I will always remember you as the glue of Johnson—the person who not only could find the answer, but who could walk you through it with patience, never with condescension or haste. You ran that office—okay, I’ll say it, you ran that school—with such grace. The culture of that school, which helped shape so many kids, shone because you chose, time and again, to go above and beyond. Happy Birthday! Matt Brunell
— Matt Brunell
🎁
Robin was the heart of Johnson. To this day, she continues to show up at events and performances all over town, because that is what a Jaguar does. We show up. She is a walking reminder of the community we loved and the spirit it embodied.
— The Pollocks
🎵
Dear Mrs. Slattery, I cannot remember a time when you were not a positive presence in my life. From fun times at pool parties when us kids were little, to you and Shannon hosting a welcome party for me when I moved across town to Kennedy, to you being an amazing, helpful, and energetic secretary (and D.J.) for my boys and all the Johnson kids. I’m so glad that Cam & Jake both got to know you more through their time at ASAP and via their standard bearer duties. You have impacted my family and so many others in countless ways. Not to mention, you created the most wonderful best friend a girl could imagine! 😊 Thank you for all that you do and all that you are. Wishing the happiest of Birthdays! Love you!
— Sarah McAuley Labrie
🤗
Thank you for always making time for us. You always have a smile for us, even though the boys were long gone from walking through the Johnson front door. Even when we see you now, always a smile, always a care for how we're all doing. So happy to have you in our lives. Thank you.
— Greg, Brinley, Sebastian, and Kai
Even Wally knew whose office this was.
Taking her calls
Checking in visitors
Johnson · Brown · Lilja · Wilson · Kennedy · NHS

Colleagues & Co-Workers

Wherever Natick Public Schools sent her next, she walked in on day one and made the whole building feel better. Every single time.

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈
🎵
Robin is always ready with a smile or warm greeting, both in person as well as over the intercom! I appreciate the personal and timely touches you add to your daily attendance emails. You always participate in spirit days, and I will never forget your St. Patrick's Day outfit!
— Jess Neel
🤗
Happy birthday, Robin! Hope you have the best day ever! I'm glad I got to know you and enjoy working with you ☺️
— Debbie I.
🎂
When I first started my job at Lilja, Robin was always so friendly and helpful to me when I was learning the Admin to the Principal role! She made me feel very welcome in the NPS community.
— Christie Ingoldsby
⭐
When I started, robin helped me with what ever I needed and made me feel part of the office staff.
— Ben
🎈
I remember working with Mrs. Slattery and Mr. Hausermann in Summer Academics, including the summer of 2020, when we had to go remote for the entire program and she was basically our third administrator. She was always so organized and responsive; we couldn't have done it without her!
— Eric Fries
💕
I absolutely loved Robin's St. Patrick's Day outfit this year. Love to see people in the office getting into the spirit! Thanks for the smile Robin!
— Ally O'Connor
🎉
Robin is the best! I'll never forget when I had a student who forgot her lunch and came down to the office in tears. Robin swooped right in and saved the day. She called home and got a hold of her "Mimi" for her and just made everything better! I was so appreciative of her in that moment!!
— Margaret Cromwell
🌟
Robin! What a gift you have: making everyone you meet feel like the most special person in the room. Every time I walked in the Johnson office you were there with your warm, happy greeting and pretending not to notice how many chocolates I took from your candy bowl. It was such a privilege working with you for some of the most special years of my life. I will leave you with the words of another Winnie (the Pooh) who once said, " I knew when I met you an adventure was going to happen!" Happy, Happy Birthday my friend! The other Winnie
— Winnie Greene
🥳
Your welcoming presence makes us comfortable and calm-- thanks for being a great host and help during your time here, and always helping the library run smoothly.
— Luke Steere
💐
Thank you for your patience and the kindness that you show to the Wilson community every day! Happy Birthday!!!
— Craig Fulton
🎊
Robin came to the HS to help out the front office during graudation time, she was a lifesaver! Robin you are the heart of the school.
— Lisa Spencer
✨
Robin, your willingness to step up in my absence will forever be appreciated. Myself & my elephant will be eternally grateful for your kindness.
— Nicole Bullock
🎶
​Dear Mrs. Slattery, It's rare to meet someone who radiates so much positivity and light wherever they go. Your kindness towards others is immeasurable, and the impact you've had in this district is truly admirable. Working with you is a genuine pleasure. Wishing you a wonderful year ahead. Happy Birthday!
— Rosie Palanukorn
💛
My daughters know Robin as the lady who gives them stickers when they came to visit me at Johnson! She always had a trinket, sticker, pencil, or piece of candy for them when they came into the office. She gave so much to all children- physical items yes, but also she gave love, comfort, and support to all. Happiest birthday Robin!
— Gina Caulfield and Family
🌸
Dear Robin, In the midst of all the stress, anxiety and chaos that constantly swirl around a middle school, your presence in the front office is so deeply appreciated. You are calm, kind, and an absolute joy to be around. Thank you for setting the tone for the whole, entire building.
— David Bernstein
🎁
Robin, You have been such a wonderful addition to Wilson. You bring such kindness and positivity to the building. I love your enthusiasm and your love of balloons and festive decorating. We need this at the middle school level. Thank you for all you have done for the students and staff in so many schools in Natick.
— Katie Harrington
🎵
Robin, I will always remember the energy and positivity you brought to Johnson School and the entire community. You celebrated every child, every adult, and every holiday to the fullest—with ALL the balloons! I’ll never forget DJ Slats blasting music and dancing on the hardtop, the amazing treats in the office, the birthday gifts for students, our lunch breaks together, that adorable pup hanging out in the summer, and all the staff celebrations we shared as friends. The way my own kids would light up when they saw you says everything—and I’ll always remember how special it was when you came to my girls’ recital to surprise them (and me). Robin, you are truly one of the most amazing, selfless people out there. I hope you have the happiest birthday and a wonderful year ahead!
— Julie Collins
🤗
Robin, I have fond memories of having you in the audience for my summer Broadway productions at Natick Summer Academics! I always enjoy seeing your smiling face- Happy Birthday!!!!!!!
— Judy Maggs
🎂
When I first started teaching at Johnson I was so nervous and Robin told my mother, who was helping me set up my classroom, that she would take good care of me and that she did.
— Meghan Schott
⭐
Remember when you made a giant batch of Jello shots to help celebrate my " Johnson School bachelor party." We took a bus ,that you organized, to Boston and had a blast! Thanks for helping make fantastic memories and taking care of us all!
— Justin Tourangeau
🎈
Dear Robin, Happy Birthday! Here’s to celebrating you not just today, but every day for the incredible person you are! You always made Johnson a better place with your humor, positivity, kindness and generosity. No matter how busy you were in the office, you always made time for anyone who walked through the door, making them feel welcome, valued and supported. You knew how to make any event special, adding decorations, sweets and balloons to every space. I was so blessed to work with you. May this milestone year bring you happiness, health, and new adventures. With Love, Jen Lipoma
— ANONYMOUS
💕
Dear Robin, Happy Birthday, Robin! I will always look back fondly on our time at Johnson and many fun moments we all had. Your kindness—especially those Honeycrisp apples, and your positive energy meant so much to me. I will definitely miss you. I wish you an amazing birthday and many more ahead. Thank you for your support and friendship during our time at Johnson.
— Terri Yee
🎉
I hope your 70th year is the best one of your life! You have spent most of your life taking care of others and now it is time to slow down and let everyone make you feel special. You deserve it more than anyone and I am happy to be a part of the spoiling Robin era! Love, Brenna
— Brenna Turner
🌟
Robin is always willing to go above and beyond to help others! She is so thoughtful and kind. When I started at Brown Elementary, Robin would often send me an email just checking in to see how everything was going and would offer help in any way. She came over to Brown one afternoon after work and walked me through step by step how to do a mail merge. She extended her knowledge as an Elementary Admin to me to help make my transition here very comfortable!
— Olivia McCall
The Ones Who Know You Best

From Friends & Family

🎈   ♪   🎈   ♫   🎈   ♩   🎈
🎵
Happy Birthday, Robin! Wishing you a fabulous 70th birthday filled with the same joy and brightness you bring to others. You are truly one of a kind. I have so enjoyed working together over the years, and I’ve been especially grateful for the privilege of calling you my sister-in-law.
— Your favorite sister-in-law :)
🤗
Wishing you the sun and the moon on your birthday. You spread the most joy and deserve the most wonderful day. Happy Birthday Robin
— jeanne
🎂
Dear Robin Jr.( inside joke) I'm thankfully that the lord put you in me and my families life. Your the person that without hesitation will give the shirt of her back even if she didn't really have it to do. The loving soul that would ask for Sky'la's meet schedule so she could go to Reggie Lewis to watch her compete. Lord knows DJ Slats loves a good time and enjoys getting her loved ones together so today we're here to celebrate you. Happy Birthday Girlfriend this is your time to shine!! Love ya
— LaKisha
⭐
Happy birthday Robin! Can you believe we are all 70 this year?! How can that be? It was just yesterday that we were driving around Natick on a Friday night looking for boys and parties! I have loved our friendship over all these years. Sharing our daughters, vacations and all that we have been through. Wishing you all the best this year and for many more to come. We have a lot more to do in our retirement!! ❤️💙
— Ann and Ralph Holmgren
🎈
Robin , i have known you since you moved to Beacon Street in i believe 8th grade ! Many wonderful times in that house !Your parents the pool ! Boys from Winthrop is a great memory! Also lots of Bruce memories! ! You have been a great friend ! And can’t believe you are 70 ! You look great ! Stay healthy and carr on 🙏💜🍀
— Susan Kelley
💕
Enjoyed the day we went to the baseball game to watch Mike play against Holliston!
— Love Janine
🎉
Dear Robin, There are so many memories! School memories, Aquinah beach, Martha's Vinyard! Katama and Nathaniels wedding, PTown for our 50th birthdays and so much more! Love you always.
— Love Janine
🌟
To the greatest mom in the world. I love you to the stars and the moon.
— Shan
🥳
To the person that showed up to our Covid wedding in a decked out car <3 (our biggest regret is not thinking to have you park and join our little festivities). We just want to say you are such an amazing human. Your thoughtfulness and kindness immediately makes any of us feel calm and happy. Thank you for being you and making our world brighter. We love you so much!
— Brian, Poli, Caiden, Sienna, and Riot
💐
Robin has been a part of our lives for a very long time! There is no one as selfless and kind as her. Making birthdays special arriving with balloons and gifts, and always a happy greeting with a hug and a smile when seen out and about. No one can put on a party spread like her! Cheers to 70 Robin, we love you! Love, The McDavitts
— The McDavitts
🎊
Happy 70th Birthday, Auntie Robin!!! I hope you know just how loved you are, not just today but every single day! You have this incredible way of bringing joy wherever you go. Your silliness, laughter, and warm spirit make every moment brighter, and it’s impossible not to have fun when you’re around! You truly are the all-star party planner, turning every gathering into something so fun and special. it only feels right that today we celebrate you, the one who has created so many amazing memories for everyone else! I’m so sad I can’t be there to celebrate in person, but please know I’m thinking of you and wishing I could give you the biggest hug! I hope your day is filled with laughter, love, and all the happiness you bring to everyone around you. Happy 70th to the most fun, joyful, and unforgettable Aunt anyone could ask for. I love you so much!!!!
— Abby, Christian, Ollie, and Ellie Mena
✨
Robin is the best neighbor anyone could ever have. I have lived next door to her and her children for 34 years..Of all the regular lovely things, the chats, the this and the that which regularly occur between people who live close together, there was one event I can never , ever, forget. On the day I lost my sweet son, I also lost my self control in that unimagineably crushing moment of immense disbelief and grief and Robin came running to hold me together as much as possible. She was still wet from a warm shower she was enjoying on that fateful Memorial Day but did not hesitate to come running to my house and hold me from collapse. I will remember this moment on that terrible day for the rest of my life. Thank you Rob...I love you dearly.
— ANONYMOUS
🎶
I am doubly blessed to have you as a neighbor and a co-worker! I will miss you when I move. I wish you the happiest of birthdays and hope you celebrate with all your family and friends! You deserve it!
— Chris Crosby
💛
Robin was best known in my house for "Sunday Funday"! Along with being a fabulous human being, she is amazing with kids. She would take Talia and Owen (my twins) out on random Sundays for an adventure which they absolutely loved. Now they are all grown up but we all cherish these memories and many others of days spent laughing and having fun with Robin.
— Jodi Feeney
🌸
To my dear second mother: When I think of you there is no one memory that comes to mind. Instead, a smile glows upon my face and a warmth in my heart. You truly are a one of a kind human and I am so blessed to have been the recipient of your love and motherhood for the majority of my life. I love you and can't wait to celebrate you.
— Jill Burdett and family
🎁
Dear Robin, We spent to much time together when the boys were growing up and playing sports. We shared so many car rides and Dunkin runs over those years. I think we knew every location near every rink, football field, soccer field and baseball field lol. We shared so many laughs and thrilling moments in our boys lives that I will always remember. You are still our dinner planner and we count on you to make sure we get together for good food and lots of laughs. Tom still talks about all sleepovers and events you planned for the gang of boys! They all think of you as a second mom because you did so much for them. You are absolutely one of the kindest, most generous and genuine people I know and I’m proud to call you my friend. Happy Birthday and I hope you are blessed with many, many more happy, healthy years and I look forward to sharing many more dinners with you! Love you, Lucy
— Lucy & Walter
🎵
🌟 Happy 70th Birthday, Mom 🌟 For 70 years you’ve made this world a better, brighter place. For 44 of those years, you’ve blessed me in ways you could never imagine — and you continue to every single day. I will never be able to thank you enough for all you do for me and for our family. I love you beyond words, Mom. Happy 70th birthday. Here’s to so many more memories still to come.
— Lainey
🤗
Dear Mrs. Slattery, As a very shy child, you were my saving grace. I don't know how I would have made it through sunshine camp without you! Growing up, you were always a friendly face to everyone around you and you spread positivity everywhere you go. I'm so glad our paths have gotten to cross so many times in this life. Thank you for spreading your joy! Sarah Frizz
— ANONYMOUS
🎂
Forever Memories for me are all the parties and weddings in our early yeard rocking out to the song, "Celebration". Had some fun with that one.
— Dan S.
⭐
There were a couple years when we were children where we celebrated new years with aunt Robin. We would go to the store and she would buy us party blowers, beads, snacks, etc… and we would stay up until midnight to watch the ball drop. Thank you for those memories aunt Robin!
— Liam Slattery
🎈
Happy 70th Birthday dear Robin .Thank you for all of your kindnesses and caring you have shown our family over these past 50 years. Especially thank you for taking Grandma Grace at age 90 plus to the Hockey Rink so she could watch your Michael play and for inviting our grandchildren Owen and Talia for those special Sunday Fun Days and most especially for just being wonderful YOU You are our family of choice Auntie Robin and we have enjoyed sharing family milestones( of which there have been many) with you. With our love and appreciation for your friendship. Love, Iris and Chuck
— Iris and Chuck Gleason
💕
Dearest Robin, You are the kindest , sweetest person I know . You are always so giving and caring. You are always there to help everyone else . You are beyond generous and so very thoughtful. You are such a beautiful soul on the inside and out. I hope you have an amazing birthday because you deserve it my dear friend ! 🥳💐🎉🎂 Lots of love , ❤️😘 Judy Goss
— Judy Goss
🎉
Dear, dear Robin, How many decades have I had the privilege to navigate the ups and downs of life in your light? I remember those days of Sarah and Laney in pre-school together, sitting on your steps with their sunglasses on ready to go... I remember Justin asking you "why are you storing food in your dishwasher" when he was a little guy and many decades later...And those magical days of: Emily, Bren, Danny and your Mike (in your care each day, over time), growing, emerging with confidence and strength...This was all before you anchored the front desk during my tenure teaching at Johnson School. How, then, does one sum up enough gratitude for the immeasurable gifts internalized by me from such a treasured relationship; from you, the Goddess of Children? With love and appreciation, Karen Cotter
— Karen Cotter and children (Jen, Justin, Sarah, Emily)
🌟
Robin, you have the biggest heart of anyone I know! So happy to have you as my sister in law. Miss all our chats sitting on Humarock Beach! Luv you!!
— Maureen
🥳
Auntie Robin, I always enjoyed the time we spent with you as kids and I still think about all the fun stuff we did to this day!! You always made our days fun and special.
— Caitlin
💐
To the most amazing woman I know! I am so proud to call you my sister and best friend. So happy to celebrate your 70th with you! All my love, Margie
— Margie Paul
🎊
Hi Robin! You have turned the big 70 years old. I hope you have a good time at the party. You deserve it. You have worked hard all your life and you have done very good with our three children. Enjoy! See you soon!
— Mike Slattery
✨
Robin is like sunshine. She lights up a room with her presence. Everyone feels happy being around her. The love she has for family and friends is abundant! She spreads joy wherever she goes. To be part of her life is a true honor and gift. We are grateful to have her in our lives for so many years. Happy 70th dear friend! We love you, Wendi and Rick ❤️🎈
— Wendi and Rick
🎵   🎈   🏫   🎂   🎶
Once a Jaguar, Always a Jaguar
Happy 70th Birthday, Robin "DJ Slats" Slattery

With love from your family, your friends, your neighbors, and every
school, rink, field, and room you've made feel like home.
Keep spinning those tunes. 💕
